finding_results.notes: The snapshot's standalone 'Are you sleeping?' line (a near-verbatim lift from Harry's style-bible example) and the narration spotlighting it have already been replaced in the current chapter by prior revision work. The sleep question now reads 'Have you slept at all this week?' integrated into the body of Harry's note, with fresh wording and no style-bible echo. The narration at line 133 characterizes Harry's directness without quoting the question back. This pass removed the softening connector 'Also, ' before the sleep question to sharpen Harry's blunt register: he drops the real question at the end of the domestic stuff without a cushioning transition, which is truer to his voice profile (direct, does not hedge). No other style-bible example lines appear verbatim or near-verbatim in the chapter's dialogue or written exchanges. Contraction levels in Harry's note and Hermione's reply are consistent with both characters' high-contraction profiles.

summary: Single finding addressed. The style-bible-derived 'Are you sleeping?' standalone line and its spotlighting narration had already been substantially reworked in a prior pass; this revision removed a remaining softening connector to lock in Harry's blunt register. No other dialogue-register, contraction-level, or example-line violations were found in the chapter's limited dialogue (two written exchanges and one letter). Chapter heading contract, setups (scar_concealment, magic_decay_baseline), and secondary-character beat (Harry's deflected concern) remain intact.
